title: Background Processing & WorkManager
description: WorkManager, foreground services, background execution limits, and choosing the right API
lesson:
Background Work in Modern Android

Android increasingly restricts background work to save battery. Understanding which API to use and when is critical for senior engineers.

Choosing the Right API

ScenarioAPIWhy
Deferrable, must completeWorkManagerSurvives process death, respects constraints
User-initiated, long-runningForeground ServiceVisible notification, elevated priority
Exact timing neededAlarmManagerPrecise scheduling (alarms, reminders)
Quick async in UI scopeCoroutinesLightweight, scoped to lifecycle

WorkManager Deep Dive

// One-time work with constraints val uploadWork = OneTimeWorkRequestBuilder<UploadWorker>() .setConstraints( Constraints.Builder() .setRequiredNetworkType(NetworkType.CONNECTED) .setRequiresBatteryNotLow(true) .build() ) .setInputData(workDataOf("imageUri" to uri.toString())) .addTag("upload") .build() WorkManager.getInstance(context).enqueue(uploadWork) // Periodic work (minimum 15 minutes) val syncWork = PeriodicWorkRequestBuilder<SyncWorker>( repeatInterval = 1, repeatIntervalTimeUnit = TimeUnit.HOURS ).build()

CoroutineWorker with Hilt

@HiltWorker class UploadWorker @AssistedInject constructor( @Assisted context: Context, @Assisted params: WorkerParameters, private val repository: UploadRepository ) : CoroutineWorker(context, params) { override suspend fun doWork(): Result { val uri = inputData.getString("imageUri") ?: return Result.failure() return try { repository.upload(uri) Result.success() } catch (e: Exception) { if (runAttemptCount < 3) Result.retry() else Result.failure() } } }

Work Chaining & Unique Work

// Chain: compress then upload then notify WorkManager.getInstance(context) .beginWith(compressWork) .then(uploadWork) .then(notifyWork) .enqueue() // Unique work — prevent duplicates WorkManager.getInstance(context).enqueueUniqueWork( "sync", ExistingWorkPolicy.KEEP, // KEEP existing, REPLACE, or APPEND syncWork )

Foreground Services (Android 14+)

Since Android 14, you must declare a foregroundServiceType in the manifest:

// AndroidManifest.xml <service android:name=".MusicService" android:foregroundServiceType="mediaPlayback" android:exported="false" /> // Available types: camera, connectedDevice, dataSync, // health, location, mediaPlayback, mediaProjection, // microphone, phoneCall, remoteMessaging, shortService, // specialUse, systemExempted
Android 15 change: dataSync foreground services are restricted to 6 hours. Google pushes apps toward WorkManager for sync tasks.

Background Execution Limits (Android 8+)

  • Service limitations — Background services are stopped minutes after the app leaves foreground
  • Implicit broadcast restrictions — Most implicit broadcasts won't reach background apps
  • Doze mode — Deep sleep with batched wake windows. Deferred alarms and jobs
  • App Standby Buckets — Active, Working Set, Frequent, Rare, Restricted — controls job frequency

questions.title: Choosing the Right API
questions.description: You need to upload photos in the background. The upload must complete even if the user kills the app, and should only run on Wi-Fi. Which API is best?
options: A foreground service with a notification
WorkManager with network constraint set to UNMETERED
viewModelScope.launch with Dispatchers.IO
AlarmManager to schedule the upload
questions.explanation: WorkManager is perfect here: it survives process death (guaranteed execution), supports constraints (Wi-Fi = NetworkType.UNMETERED), and is battery-friendly. Coroutines die with the process. AlarmManager is for time-based scheduling, not constraint-based.
hints: Which API survives process death AND respects network constraints?

questions.title: CoroutineWorker Setup
questions.description: Fill in the blanks to create a Hilt-injected WorkManager worker.
questions.explanation: @HiltWorker enables DI in Workers. @AssistedInject handles both runtime params (Context, WorkerParameters) and DI params (repository). CoroutineWorker provides a suspend doWork(). Return Result.success(), Result.failure(), or Result.retry().
hints: Workers have both runtime params and DI params
The Result sealed class has three outcomes

questions.title: Work Chain Behavior
options: uploadWork and notifyWork still execute with empty input
The entire chain is cancelled — uploadWork and notifyWork are marked CANCELLED and never run
WorkManager retries compressWork indefinitely
uploadWork runs but notifyWork is skipped
questions.explanation: WorkManager chains are sequential. If any work returns Result.failure(), all downstream work is cancelled. The chain fails fast — there's no point uploading if compression failed. For parallel work, use beginWith(listOf(work1, work2)).
hints: Think pipeline — what happens if an early stage fails?

questions.title: ExistingWorkPolicy
questions.description: You're enqueuing a unique sync job. If a sync is already running, you want to keep it and ignore the new request. Which policy?
options: ExistingWorkPolicy.REPLACE — cancel existing, start new
ExistingWorkPolicy.KEEP — keep existing, ignore new request
ExistingWorkPolicy.APPEND — chain new after existing
ExistingWorkPolicy.UPDATE — update existing work's parameters
questions.explanation: KEEP keeps the existing work and ignores the new enqueue. REPLACE cancels running work and starts fresh. APPEND chains the new work after existing. Choose based on whether duplicate work matters.
hints: You don't want to interrupt an ongoing sync

questions.title: Foreground Service Crash on Android 14
questions.description: This foreground service crashes on Android 14+ with MissingForegroundServiceTypeException. Fix it.
questions.explanation: Android 14 requires both: (1) android:foregroundServiceType in the manifest, and (2) passing the type to startForeground(). Without these, the system throws MissingForegroundServiceTypeException. You also need the corresponding permission (e.g., ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION).
hints: Android 14 added a new requirement for foreground services
The type must be declared in TWO places

questions.title: Observing Work Progress
questions.description: Write code to observe an upload worker's progress in a ViewModel and expose it as a StateFlow. The worker reports progress as an integer (0-100).
questions.explanation: setProgress() reports progress via Data objects. The ViewModel observes with getWorkInfoByIdFlow() which returns a Flow of WorkInfo updates containing progress, state, and output data.
hints: setProgress takes a Data object, same format as inputData

questions.title: Doze Mode Impact
questions.description: What happens to a periodic WorkManager job when the device enters Doze mode?
options: The job is cancelled permanently
The job runs normally — WorkManager ignores Doze
The job is deferred until a maintenance window, then executes
The job is converted to an expedited job automatically
questions.explanation: In Doze mode, the system batches all deferred work into periodic maintenance windows. WorkManager respects Doze — it defers jobs but guarantees they eventually run. This saves battery while ensuring reliability.
hints: Doze batches work to save battery, not cancels it

questions.title: Expedited Work
options: For all background work to make it faster
For time-sensitive tasks that need immediate execution (like sending a message), with graceful fallback when quota is exhausted
For periodic work that must run at exact intervals
For work that must survive device reboot
questions.explanation: Expedited work gets priority execution — starts immediately with elevated process priority. Use it for user-initiated, time-sensitive tasks. RUN_AS_NON_EXPEDITED falls back gracefully when the system's expedited quota is exhausted. Don't overuse it — the system limits expedited work per app.
hints: Expedited = higher priority, but the system limits how much you can use
